initiativeCore.problemStatement: Users lack a seamless, accessible way to track daily habits and personal goals with timely reminders and visual progress tracking across multiple communication channels.
goals: Enable users to track daily habits and personal goals
Provide customizable reminders to support habit consistency
Visualize habit-building progress through intuitive charts
Deliver streak notifications to motivate continued engagement
Integrate with SMS, Telegram, and WhatsApp for multi-channel accessibility
Support bi-directional communication allowing users to mark habits complete via messaging platforms
nonGoals: Social features or habit sharing between users (not in initial scope)
Advanced analytics or AI-powered habit recommendations (not in initial scope)
Integration with additional messaging platforms beyond SMS, Telegram, and WhatsApp
initiativeCore.businessImpact: Success: Increased user engagement and retention through habit consistency features; potential for premium subscription revenue from advanced features. Failure: Low adoption if reminders are unreliable or integrations fail; user churn if progress tracking lacks value.
initiativeCore.stakeholders: Unknown - no stakeholders identified in brief

functional.description: Users can create and manage custom daily habits (maximum 5 habits per user)
Users can set and customize reminder schedules for each habit with flexible frequencies (daily, every 2 days, weekly, monthly, hourly intervals: 1h, 6h)
System displays progress charts visualizing habit completion over time
System sends streak notifications when users maintain habit consistency
System integrates with SMS for bi-directional reminder delivery and habit completion
System integrates with Telegram for bi-directional reminder delivery and habit completion
System integrates with WhatsApp for bi-directional reminder delivery and habit completion
Users can mark habits as complete for each day via web interface
Users can define personal goals associated with habits
System tracks and displays current streak count per habit
Users can mark habits as complete by replying to reminder messages on SMS, Telegram, or WhatsApp
System enforces maximum limit of 5 habits per user
System supports multiple reminder frequency options: daily, every 2 days, weekly, monthly, every 1 hour, every 6 hours
System parses and processes incoming messages from users to mark habits complete
System sends confirmation messages when habits are marked complete via messaging platforms

nonFunctional.description: Reminder delivery must be timely and reliable across all messaging platforms
User interface must be intuitive and require minimal learning curve
Progress charts must load and render within 2 seconds
System must be accessible on mobile and desktop web browsers
Platform must handle concurrent users without performance degradation
User data must be securely stored and transmitted
Bi-directional message processing must occur within 5 seconds of user reply
System must handle message parsing errors gracefully and provide helpful feedback

edgeCases: User changes timezone while active streaks are in progress
Messaging platform API is temporarily unavailable during scheduled reminder time
User has multiple habits with overlapping reminder times
User deletes a habit with an active streak
User receives reminders across multiple platforms simultaneously
User's phone number or messaging account becomes invalid
Streak calculation when user marks habit complete after midnight
Chart rendering with incomplete or sparse habit data
User attempts to create duplicate habits
Rate limiting from messaging platform APIs during high-volume reminder periods
User attempts to create more than 5 habits
User replies with ambiguous or unrecognized text to mark habit complete
User marks same habit complete multiple times in same period
Reminder frequency conflicts (e.g., hourly reminders overlapping with daily reminders)
User replies to old reminder messages after new ones have been sent
Bi-directional message processing when webhook endpoints are down
User switches messaging platforms mid-streak
Parsing replies in different languages or with typos
Handling rapid-fire replies from users across multiple habits
Monthly reminder scheduling across months with different day counts

criteria.description: User can successfully create a new habit with custom name and reminder schedule from available frequency options
User receives reminder notification via selected messaging platform at scheduled time
Progress chart displays accurate completion data for the past 30 days
Streak notification is sent when user completes habit for 3 consecutive days
User can mark a habit as complete and see updated streak count immediately
User interface is intuitive and easy to navigate
All three messaging platforms (SMS, Telegram, WhatsApp) successfully deliver reminders
User can customize reminder frequency and time for each habit independently using supported frequencies
App empowers users to cultivate positive habits effortlessly
User can reply to reminder message on SMS, Telegram, or WhatsApp to mark habit complete
System prevents user from creating more than 5 habits and displays appropriate error message
System correctly processes bi-directional messages and updates habit status within 5 seconds
User receives confirmation message after successfully marking habit complete via messaging platform
System supports all specified reminder frequencies: daily, every 2 days, weekly, monthly, every 1 hour, every 6 hours

measurableOutcomes: Percentage of users who maintain 7-day streaks
Reminder delivery success rate per messaging platform
Average number of habits tracked per active user (target: 3-5)
Time to complete first habit creation flow
Percentage of habits marked complete via messaging vs web interface
Average response time for bi-directional message processing

internalDependencies: Backend infrastructure for habit data storage and retrieval
Webhook infrastructure for receiving incoming messages
Message parsing and natural language processing logic

externalDependencies.name: SMS Gateway Provider (e.g., Twilio, AWS SNS)
Messaging platform rate limits and quotas
SMS provider inbound message processing capabilities

factors: Critical sections missing: stakeholders, timeline, budget, constraints
No defined success metrics or KPIs beyond basic outcomes
No stakeholder identification or approval process
No timeline or delivery milestones specified
No budget constraints or resource allocation defined
High dependency on external messaging APIs with potential rate limits and costs
Bi-directional messaging adds complexity to webhook infrastructure and message parsing
No technical constraints or architecture decisions documented
Unclear scope boundaries for MVP features beyond core functionality
No rollout or phasing strategy defined
Potential compliance requirements for SMS/messaging not addressed
Message parsing complexity with multiple languages and formats
Webhook reliability concerns for bi-directional communication
Cost implications of bi-directional SMS messaging not assessed
mitigations: Define clear MVP scope with prioritized features (5 habit limit reduces initial complexity)
Establish stakeholder review and approval process
Document technical architecture and API integration strategy for bi-directional messaging
Create detailed timeline with milestones and dependencies
Assess messaging platform costs including bi-directional SMS charges and rate limits before commitment
Define measurable success criteria and KPIs
Identify compliance requirements for messaging integrations
Establish fallback mechanisms for messaging platform failures and webhook downtime
Define budget for external API usage including inbound message processing
Implement robust message parsing with clear user instructions and error handling
Design webhook infrastructure with retry logic and monitoring
Create simple, standardized reply formats to reduce parsing complexity
Implement rate limiting and queue management for bi-directional messages
Test bi-directional flows extensively across all three platforms before launch

deliveryIntent.rolloutExpectations: Unknown - no rollout strategy defined in brief
deliveryIntent.timelineAssumptions: Unknown - no timeline provided in brief
constraints: Maximum 5 habits per user to limit initial complexity and API costs
Bi-directional messaging must be supported across all three platforms (SMS, Telegram, WhatsApp)
Reminder frequencies limited to: daily, every 2 days, weekly, monthly, every 1 hour, every 6 hours
Social features explicitly excluded from initial scope
deliveryIntent.budget: Unknown - no budget specified in brief

No compliance or regulatory requirements addressed for messaging

No competitive analysis or market positioning

No API cost estimates or usage projections for bi-directional messaging
No disaster recovery or failover strategy
No accessibility standards or requirements defined
No internationalization or localization requirements for message parsing
No performance benchmarks or SLAs specified beyond chart load time
No message parsing language support defined
No webhook infrastructure requirements or SLAs specified
